posts.hook: Week 2 done. Your eyes worked hard. Here's the 1-minute recovery.
posts.caption:
End of Week 2.

Your ciliary muscles have been contracting non-stop since Monday.

Right now:
1. Warm your palms — rub together 10 seconds
2. Cup gently over your closed eyes
3. Hold 60 seconds. Breathe. Darkness.

This is palming. Optometrists call it the most underrated eye exercise.

Guided version: axeo.vision/exercise

#Palming #EyeExercise #WeekRecap #Axeo #HealthTok #EyeHealth

posts.hook: Palming — the 60-second exercise that resets your eyes after a full week.
posts.caption:
Palming is the oldest eye exercise in the book.

Here's why it works:
- Darkness eliminates all visual stimulation
- Warmth from hands increases local circulation
- Ciliary muscle fully releases its near-focus contraction
- Retinal cells get a chance to restore

How to do it:
1. Rub palms together briskly (10 sec)
2. Cup over closed eyes — no pressure on eyeballs
3. Hold 60–90 seconds
4. Breathe slowly

Best time: before bed, after long work sessions.

Guided version with timer: axeo.vision/exercise

#Palming #EyeExercise #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #NaturalEyeCare #VisionTherapy

posts.hook: Week 2 eye health summary — everything that matters in 30 seconds.
posts.caption:
Week 2 — what we covered:

🔵 Myopia epidemic (1 in 3 globally → 1 in 2 by 2050)
🔵 Kids: myopia onset 2 years earlier than 1990
🔵 Convergence insufficiency — affects 1 in 10
🔵 Blue light glasses: not evidence-based
🔵 Blink rate on screens: 66% lower than normal
🔵 Astigmatism: 1 in 3 adults, often undiagnosed

Free tools for all of the above: axeo.vision

posts.hook: Weekly check-in: what eye health habit are you actually keeping from this week?
posts.caption:
r/EyeCare or r/HealthyHabits

End of another week. Trying a simple check-in:

What's the one eye health habit you actually maintained this week?

For me it was the 20-20-20 rule — set a timer every 20 minutes on my work computer. Managed about 60% of the time which I'm calling a win.

Also tested my visual acuity and contrast sensitivity on axeo.vision (free browser tool, no signup). Contrast sensitivity improved slightly from last week — small but encouraging.

Curious what's working for others. No judgment on frequency — any habit is better than none.
